creator: riggs@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 25 May 2014 02:20, Ronald F. Guilmette <rfg@tristatelogic.com> wrote:
> 1)  As far as I can recall, I never manually disabled the FriBiDi
> build option.  I may just have forgotten that I did that, at some
> point in the long history of my personal ports tree, but I really
> don't think I did.  Why would I have done?

The fribidi option was disabled by default in the past.
If you had built the port before, it saved its build options (which
was without fribidi).
Look at the mplayer build config, which should be located in
/var/db/ports/multimedia_mplayer/options

As you see, the file maintains a complete list of all options. This is
the reason why you get the config dialogue each time an option is
added or removed. However it does not save the default config at build
time, so you don't get the option dialogue automatically when a
pre-existing default setting has changed.

> 2)  I will be more than happy to check for you to make sure that
> the FriBiDi build option is enabled in a default set of build options
> for mplayer, if you will just be kind enough to answer one question
> for me:  How does one effectively reset all build options to the
> distributed defaults?  Will "make clean" do that, or is something
> else needed?  (Sorry, this is probably an FAQ, but I've never known
> the answer.)

You could just remove the build config file mentioned above if you like.
Recommended, using make:
cd /usr/ports/multimedia/mplayer
make rmconfig
Or, if you want to remove the config for the port itself and all its
dependencies:
make rmconfig-recursive

If you are interested, the default Make targets for the build process
have a nice, brief description in
/usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk
search for "Default targets and their behaviors"

Best regards
Riggs
